decisions.title: Project pivots from Vega-game-engine to RTS-for-agent-orchestration
decisions.one_line: Abandon Vega-specific game design; reconceive as RTS-style operator interface for controlling autonomous coding agents.

decisions.title: Agents modeled as hypergraph edge authors, not spatial entities
decisions.one_line: Shift from 'where are agents on a map' to 'what edges agents create in a temporal multiplex graph'.

decisions.title: Six-tier architecture diagram committed
decisions.one_line: RTS core + integrations + isolation + agent model crystallized into a single architecture stack.

decisions.title: Per-zone MCP server instances chosen over host-shared servers
decisions.one_line: MCP servers run inside each zone by default to preserve isolation; commons servers reserved for cheap shared tools.

decisions.title: V1 scope reduction: cut 3D/VR, macOS, SSH, MCP, bubblewrap, live policy, event sourcing, NATO, VegaFusion, magic CLI
decisions.one_line: Strip the architecture down to the minimum that tests the hypothesis that RTS metaphor improves agent oversight.

decisions.title: Reject React; reframe frontend as not the load-bearing question
decisions.one_line: Frontend framework choice is downstream; the real differentiator is the chat/operator interaction model.

decisions.title: VS Code adopted as unified host platform
decisions.one_line: Drop browser-based chat; deliver as a VS Code extension to eliminate context-switching and inherit rich APIs.

decisions.title: MCP used bidirectionally as primary agent communication channel
decisions.one_line: Operator becomes an MCP server; agent communicates via MCP tool calls (clarify, request_approval, notify) instead of free text.

decisions.title: VS Code NotebookController API used; no Jupyter kernel, no Python runtime
decisions.one_line: Keep cell paradigm and notebook UI; drop the kernel entirely so there is no protocol bridge or Python expectation collision.

decisions.title: Agent text output suppressed; tool calls become sole communication
decisions.one_line: Forced tool use migrates context entirely into structured MCP calls; no free-form agent prose in the operator surface.

decisions.title: Subtractive fork of vscode-jupyter
decisions.one_line: Remove Python integration, IPyWidgets, remote servers, debugging, and viewers; keep notebook editor and cell semantics.

decisions.title: LLMKernel hardcoded as sole kernel; no kernel discovery
decisions.one_line: Cut kernel selection ceremony; one kernel is wired in by default.

decisions.title: V1 scope confirmed feasible with Claude Code as collaborator
decisions.one_line: Honest feasibility assessment: fork/merge LLMKernel, advanced chat UI, and RTS view are deliverable as a serious V1.

decisions.title: Three storage structures (layout tree, agent graph, chat flow) embedded in single .llmnb file
decisions.one_line: Each structure uses the data shape that fits its data; all three serialize as JSON inside one ipynb-derived file.

decisions.title: Bidirectional 'paper-telephone' MCP between kernel and extension
decisions.one_line: LLMKernel and the VS Code extension both expose MCP tool surfaces to each other; the kernel mediates MCP and PTY traffic.

decisions.title: Bell System-inspired standards discipline: RFC-driven tool specs and provisioning
decisions.one_line: Tool specifications, provisioning procedures, and reliability practices are written as numbered RFCs before implementation, mirroring Bell System engineering culture.
